title: I built an automated trading bot for Polymarket using Kelly Criterion + Bayesian Ensemble — open source
author: Subject_Zone_9789
selftext:
Been building this for a few months. SharkFlow is a Python bot for Polymarket (prediction markets) that combines several math models to find edges:

What it does:

\- Kelly Criterion (25% fractional) + Expected Value filter — only bets when EV > 5%

\- Bayesian Ensemble with Platt/Beta/Isotonic calibration

\- Rush Mode: 5-minute BTC windows using RSI + crowd consensus as contrarian signals

\- Whale tracker — monitors large wallets for signal confirmation

\- Dual-AI analysis (Claude + OpenAI) for high-return opportunities

\- Bloomberg terminal-style dashboard



Stack: Python, FastAPI, scikit-learn, httpx, WebSockets



Still refining the edge — prediction markets are surprisingly efficient. Happy to discuss the math behind it.

GitHub: [https://github.com/ddchack/sharkflow](https://github.com/ddchack/sharkflow)

comments.author: RichSails-
comments.body: Been building bots over the last couple of months. It has to be the way forward. Love further info.
